caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5614 occupations are a poor proxy for 561492 and omit court reporters/stenographers.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Freelance and 1099 reporter payouts may be a larger production cost than the W-2 wage-based labor-share input.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The role arithmetic is required against the supplied proxy and is not observed 561492 occupational data.
inputs.pi_dist: Federal court rates are a pricing-mechanism reference, not a complete representation of private deposition pricing.
inputs.pi_moat: No target-specific reporter agreements, rule coverage or client retention data was reviewed.
inputs.t50_years: AAERT documents a digital path, not a measured 561492 AI-adoption rate.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is effective-work adoption, not the percentage of firms using any AI tool.
inputs.historical_analogs: Digital reporting is not equivalent to fully autonomous certification.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Reporter age is not the same as business-owner age.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is evidence of a reporter pipeline shortage, not a census of agency ownership succession plans.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a paid transaction-database count and active status varies by geography and target quality.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 is a commercial valuation guide rather than a disclosed-comps database; the dataset's $1-10M EBITDA band spans regional and platform categories.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit depends on client/roster transferability and is not a guaranteed multiple expansion; source ranges are commercial estimates.
inputs.pricing_structure: Federal court schedules do not establish a universal private deposition price or agency margin.

Validator caveat: reporter retirement and production-talent shortage are proxies for agency-owner age and ownership succession; this modestly raises B but does not change the kill verdict or conditional thesis.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $242,882 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561492); class <5: 2,701 firms, avg revenue/firm $234,096 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $36,636 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 164 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,455,720 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $227,820 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 72 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,802,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $438,513 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 51 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,039,745 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,101,720 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $46,248,556 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,237,899 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 7 firms, avg revenue/firm $182,932,429 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $28,628,925 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 60

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI/digital workflows materially expose transcription, coordination, QA and routine administration, while certified capture, legal accountability and realtime delivery retain a human floor. Because the supplied 4-digit role proxy omits court reporters, the aggregate intentionally remains conservative and low-confidence.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561492 ($800,378,213) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561492 ($3,128,565,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact-artifact identity, source coverage and arithmetic pass. The sources support the industry boundary, digital-reporting pathway, pricing mechanism, commercial multiple ranges and consolidation direction. The owner/succession use of reporter-workforce evidence is a non-fatal disclosed proxy that modestly affects B but cannot change the kill verdict or useful conditional thesis; no material numeric-support, factor-direction or capture/terminal double-counting defect was found.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 561492 Court Reporting and Stenotype Services
sources.what_it_supports: Exact NAICS scope: verbatim reporting and stenotype recording of live legal proceedings plus transcription of recorded materials; the code includes realtime captioning examples.

sources.title: AAERT: About AAERT Certifications
sources.what_it_supports: Digital reporting and transcription certification pathway, real-time audio monitoring, and the association's description of stenographer shortages and digital reporting as a scalable alternative.

sources.title: Northern District of California: Transcript Pricing Explanation & Examples
sources.what_it_supports: Primary federal-court examples of per-page transcript pricing and delivery/copy-rate distinctions; court pricing is not a complete proxy for private deposition pricing.

sources.title: Court Reporting Firm Valuation: 4.5-10.5x EBITDA (2026)
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial valuation guide's stated 2026 ranges for regional and platform court-reporting firms, used as a disclosed third-party range rather than as a transaction database.

sources.title: Jackim Woods: Court Reporting Acquisitions in 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial M&A advisory source describing court-reporting consolidation and listing 2025 acquisitions by national and regional buyers.

title: Court Reporting and Stenotype Services
